pattern: A round trip is equivalent to two simultaneous top-left to bottom-right walks, so a shared-step dynamic program over both walkers' positions maximizes cherries while never double-counting a shared cell.
hints: Going there and back is the same as sending two people from the start to the end at the same time, each only moving right or down.
Both walkers take the same number of steps, so a single counter ties their positions together: knowing three of the four coordinates fixes the fourth.

Define dp by step count and the two row positions; add cherries from both cells (once if they coincide), block paths through thorns, and take the best of the four move combinations, clamping the start cell to a valid path.

edge_inputs.description: no path through is possible, zero cherries
thorn forces one route, second pass picks remainder
row grid forces the same single path both ways

stress.description: small random grids that keep the corners passable and avoid full blockage
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 8))
    while True:
        g = [[rng.choice([-1, 0, 1, 1]) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
        g[0][0] = 1
        g[n - 1][n - 1] = 1
        return [g]
